name: Mandelbreit
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the almond paste, sugar, salt, butter, shortening, and eggs. Beat until smooth.
2. Add flour, baking powder, vanilla extract, and almond extract to the mixture. Mix well until a dough forms.
3. Place the dough on a floured surface and knead a few times until a smooth ball is formed.
4. Divide the dough into three pieces.
5. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
6. To prepare the filling, mix cocoa powder, sugar, and water in a bowl until a paste forms.
7. Roll out one piece of dough to a rectangle measuring approximately 24 x 6 inches.
8. Spread the filling along the center of the rolled dough in a long ribbon.
9. Wrap the dough around the filling and shape into a smooth roll.
10. Cut the roll into two lengths, each about 12 inches long, and place them on a greased cookie sheet, spacing them about 4 inches apart.
11. Repeat steps 5-10 with the remaining pieces of dough, spreading walnuts for one piece and candied fruits for another.
12. Brush all rolls with the egg wash and sprinkle with additional sugar.
13. Bake the six rolls in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until they are richly browned. Allow to cool on wire racks.
14. Cut the rolls into 1-inch pieces by slicing crosswise. The Mandelbreit will taste even better after aging for one week wrapped in foil. Slice after unwrapping.
